summary: Backend + Frontend testing post Next.js 14 migration. Found CRITICAL missing JWT_SECRET env var that broke ALL auth (500 errors). Added it to /app/backend/.env to unblock testing. After fix: 64/64 backend pytest tests pass. All 11 frontend routes (incl. deep refresh /men/product/1, /admin/inventory etc.) load 200 with no JS/console errors. Login + admin dashboard + all 8 admin sub-pages render correctly. Next.js catch-all routing works as designed.

critical.issue: JWT_SECRET env var was missing from /app/backend/.env causing KeyError -> 500 on ALL /api/auth/* endpoints (login, register, logout, refresh) and any auth-protected route. Login/Register/Cart/Wishlist/Account/Admin/Inventory all unusable until fixed. I added a dev value to unblock; main agent should set a strong production value via Emergent secrets and document.

issues: Hero slide #1 shows a clearly visible NIKE shoe with Nike swoosh logo on a luxury Cobblyn brand site - trademark/brand-mismatch risk. Replace with neutral/branded imagery.

action_items: Set JWT_SECRET in backend/.env (or Emergent secret) with a strong random value; do not rely on tester-added dev value.
Add a defensive default or fail-fast log message in auth_utils.get_jwt_secret() so missing config produces a clear startup error rather than runtime 500.
Replace Nike-branded hero image on home slider with brand-safe imagery.
(Existing pytest suite uses BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env which must be exported when running pytest; consider loading frontend/.env or backend/.env in conftest for convenience.)
critical_code_review_comments: auth_utils.get_jwt_secret uses os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] (raises KeyError) - any missing config = 500 to user. Should validate at startup.
Next.js migration via app/[[...slug]]/page.js with ssr:false is sound and works for all deep routes incl. /admin/* and /men/product/:id refreshes.
api.js correctly prefers NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL fallback - good.
Backend seed runs on each startup and is idempotent (logs 'already seeded') - good.
updated_files: /app/backend/.env (added JWT_SECRET dev value to unblock auth testing)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on route loading + login + admin nav

test_credentials: Admin admin@cobblyn.com / Cobblyn@2026 (works). Test user test@cobblyn.com / Test@1234 created via register.
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 14 products, 11 materials, 5 pricing rules, inventory; pytest fixtures register TEST_ users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: JWT_SECRET issue was fixed by tester. If backend reverts/redeploys, re-add JWT_SECRET to /app/backend/.env. Pytest must be run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL exported: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://proto-enhance-1.preview.emergentagent.com pytest backend/tests/`. All routes verified loading; functional flows beyond login (cart add, checkout, customize price calc, ticket creation) were not exercised in UI but corresponding backend endpoints all pass pytest.
rca of the issue: RCA: /app/backend/auth_utils.py line 12 does `os.environ['JWT_SECRET']`. backend/.env shipped only MONGO_URL, DB_NAME, CORS_ORIGINS - JWT_SECRET key missing => KeyError on first auth call => FastAPI returns generic 500 'Internal Server Error'. Repro: POST /api/auth/login with admin creds before fix returns 500. Fix: append JWT_SECRET=... to backend/.env and supervisorctl restart backend. Mitigation: validate required env at FastAPI startup (raise on missing) and load via pydantic Settings.
